sources: Tax Foundation 2026 State Income Tax Rates
OBBBA (One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed July 4 2025)
_meta.disclaimer: For informational purposes. Verify with IRS.gov or a qualified tax professional before filing.
_meta.patchNotes: v2.0 patch: Added 1 gig profile (babysitter_nanny_1099), 5 creator profiles (ugc_creator, online_course_creator, newsletter_business, sponsorship_income, brand_deal_calculator), 3 seller profiles (facebook_marketplace, stockx_goat, printful_printify), 9 rental types (getaround, rv_rental, boat_rental, equipment_rental, parking_space_rental, storage_rental, short_vs_long_term_rental, landlord, real_estate_agent_rental), 11 professional profiles (bookkeeper_accountant, virtual_assistant, coach, insurance_agent, notary_signing_agent, tutor, wedding_event_planner, qa_tester_contractor, clergy_minister, farm_schedule_f), 7 entity types (limitedPartnership, professionalCorporation, familyLLC, holdingCompany, realEstateHoldingLLC, seriesLLC, statutoryEmployee).
_meta.patchNotes2_1: v2.1 patch: (1) Normalized farm_schedule_f.uniqueDeductions to match {name, deductible, note} schema used by otherCommonDeductions. Added _deductionSchema hint to both arrays. Ensured all otherCommonDeductions items have deductible and note fields to prevent undefined exceptions in UI parser. (2) Enhanced husbandWifeQualifiedJointVenture with community property state list, LLC registration restriction rule, stateOverlayValidation engine object with per-state routing logic, and comparison to partnership filing. (3) Added top-level stateMetadata object with communityPropertyStates, noIncomeTaxStates, statesThatTaxSocialSecurity, statesWithLocalIncomeTax, statesWithSDI, and passthroughEntityTaxStates for SALT workaround states.
_meta.patchNotes3_0: v3.0: Added equityCompensation (RSU, ISO, NSO, ESPP, QSBS, phantom/SARs, Form 1099-B basis reconciliation, multi-state allocation). Added combinedSalaryCalculator with all income type definitions, 5 family household scenarios, quarterly estimated tax engine with safe harbor, annualized income method, withholding optimization tool. Updated seoMetadata with equity keyword cluster and 14 new page patterns.

fixes.source: IRS Notice 2025-67 via White Coat Investor, ALPA
IRS Notice 2025-67 via White Coat Investor, ALPA
SSA.gov 2026 COLA Fact Sheet, Atticus.com, DisabilityExchange.org

fixes.correctedTo: Full fee schedule per corp.delaware.gov: Authorized Shares min $175, Assumed Par Value min $400, filing fee $50, late penalty $200 + 1.5%/mo, Large Corporate Filer max $250,000

seniorDeductionOBBBA.note: New OBBBA 2025 deduction for age 65+. Separate from additional standard deduction.

niit.note: Not indexed for inflation. Unchanged since 2013.

additionalMedicare.note: Applies to wages and SE income over threshold. Not indexed for inflation.

selfEmployment.note: SE tax = seTaxRate x (netEarnings x netEarningsMultiplier). Half of SE tax is deductible from AGI.

safeHarborRules.rule1: Pay 90% of current year tax liability
safeHarborRules.rule2: Pay 100% of prior year tax liability (110% if prior year AGI > $150,000)

childTaxCredit.note: OBBBA permanently extended CTC. Refundable portion (ACTC) up to $1,700.

earnedIncomeCredit.note: MFJ phaseout starts $5,990 higher than single/hoh.

childDependentCareCredit.note: Non-refundable. Rate phases down from 35% to 20% as income rises above $15,000.

saltDeduction.note: OBBBA raised SALT cap from $10,000 to $40,400 (2026-2029). Phases out for high earners.

mortgageInterestDeduction.note: Only for loans originated after Dec 15, 2017. Older loans still use $1M limit.

foreignEarnedIncomeExclusion.note: For US citizens/residents working abroad who meet bona fide residence or physical presence test.

solo401k.employerLimit: 25% of net self-employment income (approx 20% before SE tax deduction)
solo401k.note: Employee deferral deadline: Dec 31. Employer contribution deadline: tax filing deadline + extensions.

sep_ira.filingDeadline: Tax return due date including extensions (Oct 15, 2027)
sep_ira.note: 25% of W-2 compensation or 20% of net SE income (after SE tax deduction), whichever is less.

traditional_ira.note: IRA catch-up increased to $1,100 in 2026 — first increase since 2006 (SECURE 2.0 inflation indexing)

roth_ira.backdoorNote: If over income limit, use backdoor Roth (non-deductible traditional IRA then convert). Pro-rata rule applies.

hsa.note: Triple tax advantage: deductible contribution, tax-free growth, tax-free withdrawals for medical. HSA funds can invest like IRA after minimum cash balance met.
hsa._correctionNote: Corrected June 2026: previous values ($4,300/$8,550) were 2025 figures. 2026 limits per IRS Notice 2025-67 are $4,400 (self-only) and $8,750 (family). Source: White Coat Investor, IRS Notice 2025-67, ALPA benefits summary.

definedBenefit.maxContributionEstimate: Varies by age and actuarial factors. Can exceed $200,000/yr for older high earners.
definedBenefit.note: Requires actuary. Best for high earners age 50+ with stable high income.

mileage.note: Business rate is 72.5 cents (up from 70 cents in 2025). Cannot use standard mileage if previously claimed MACRS depreciation on vehicle.

simplified.note: No depreciation recapture on home sale. Simpler recordkeeping.

actual.method: Divide home office sq ft by total home sq ft. Apply percentage to: mortgage interest or rent, utilities, insurance, repairs, depreciation.
actual.note: Depreciation recapture (25%) applies when home is sold. Higher deduction for most situations.

homeOffice.rule: Space must be used regularly and exclusively for business. Home office must be principal place of business.

section179.note: OBBBA made 100% bonus depreciation permanent for qualified property placed in service after Jan 19, 2025.

vehicleActualMethod.approach: Track actual gas, insurance, repairs, registration, depreciation x business-use percentage

macrsDepreciationCar.note: Annual luxury auto limits. Apply business-use % to these caps.

augustaRule.taxTreatment: Rental income from personal residence is excluded from gross income (not reported). Business deducts rent as ordinary business expense.
notEligible: Single-member LLC taxed as disregarded entity
requirements: Must be fair market rate (document with Peerspace, hotel, coworking comparables)
Must have written rental agreement between you and the entity
Must have documented business purpose (board meeting, retreat, planning session)
Cannot also deduct home office for same space on same days
Cannot be primary place of business
augustaRule.maxTaxFreeSavings: Days x FMR daily rate (up to 14 days). Tax-free to owner. Deductible to entity.

selfEmployedHealthInsurance.notAvailableIf: Eligible for employer-subsidized plan through spouse's employer
selfEmployedHealthInsurance.sCorpNote: S Corp owner-employees include health insurance in W-2 Box 1 wages, then deduct as SE health insurance on 1040.

requirements: Document: who, what, where, business discussed
mealsDeduction.note: 100% deductible for food at a restaurant provided to employee on employer premises (company cafeteria). 50% for client meals and business meals away.

truckDriverPerDiem.note: $80/day for OTR truck drivers (DOT regulated). 80% deductible. $64/day net deduction.

businessTravel.spouseTravelRule: Spouse travel deductible ONLY if spouse is employee of business and travel has bona fide business purpose.

qualifiedBusinessIncomeDeduction.sstbNote: Specified Service Trades or Businesses (health, law, consulting, athletics, financial services, performing arts) — QBI fully phases out for SSTBs above phaseout range.
qualifiedBusinessIncomeDeduction.wageLimit: At higher incomes, QBI limited to greater of: 50% of W-2 wages paid, or 25% of W-2 wages + 2.5% of qualified property
qualifiedBusinessIncomeDeduction.note: QBI deduction applies to sole props, partnerships, S corps, trusts. Does NOT apply to C corps or employee wages.

hireChildren.ficaExemptAppliesTo: Sole proprietor and husband/wife partnership only
hireChildren.maxReasonableWages: Must be real work at fair market rate

benefits: Spouse eligible for employer retirement plan
Spouse builds own Social Security credits
hireSpouse.sole_prop_note: Sole prop employing spouse: FUTA does NOT apply to spouse wages. State unemployment varies.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Professional development
otherCommonDeductions.note: Must maintain/improve current business skills. Not qualifying for new career.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Software and subscriptions
otherCommonDeductions.note: R&D software can be fully expensed in year of cost under OBBBA.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Startup costs
otherCommonDeductions.note: $5,000 deductible in year 1. Remainder amortized over 180 months.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Qualified retirement plan contributions
otherCommonDeductions.note: Solo 401k, SEP-IRA, SIMPLE. See retirement section.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Uniforms/protective clothing
otherCommonDeductions.note: Must not be suitable for everyday wear. Work boots, hard hats, lab coats OK. Business casual not deductible.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Tools and equipment (under Section 179)
otherCommonDeductions.note: Up to $1,220,000. Must be >50% business use.

otherCommonDeductions.name: Charitable contributions
otherCommonDeductions.note: NOT deductible on Schedule C. Deductible only as itemized deduction on Schedule A.

selfEmploymentDeductions._deductionSchema: All deduction arrays use: { name:string, deductible:number (1.0=100%, 0.5=50%, 0=not deductible, dollar_amount=capped), note:string }. Access as item.deductible ?? 1.0 and item.note ?? '' to handle legacy entries safely.

soleProprietorship.setup: None required. Auto-created by doing business.
soleProprietorship.bestFor: Net profit under $60,000. Testing a business idea. Very low compliance burden.
soleProprietorship.drawback: Highest effective tax rate. No liability protection.

singleMemberLLC.setup: File Articles of Organization with state. EIN recommended.
singleMemberLLC.bestFor: Sole proprietor wanting liability protection. Same tax as sole prop until election.
singleMemberLLC.note: Disregarded for federal tax but treated as separate legal entity for liability.

multiMemberLLC.taxForm: Form 1065 + Schedule K-1 to each member
multiMemberLLC.seTaxApplies: On each member's distributive share (general partners)
multiMemberLLC.setup: Articles of Organization + Operating Agreement required
multiMemberLLC.bestFor: Two or more owners. Flexible profit/loss allocation in operating agreement.

llcElectingScorp.seTaxApplies: Only on W-2 salary, not distributions
llcElectingScorp.electionForm: Form 2553 (file by March 15 for current year, or within 75 days of formation)
llcElectingScorp.sCorpSalarySweetSpot: 35–45% of net profit as salary. Lower salary = more QBI + less FICA, but IRS audit risk if too low.
complianceItems: Payroll (run actual payroll, not owner draws)

sCorporation.note: Same tax treatment as LLC electing S corp. Different formation path (incorporate first, then elect S corp status).
sCorporation.eligibleShareholders: US citizens/residents only. No corporate shareholders.

cCorporation.doubleTaxation: Corp pays 21% on profits. Dividends paid to shareholders taxed again at 0–20% qualified dividend rate.
cCorporation.bestFor: VC-backed startups, high retained earnings, benefits-heavy compensation, stock options (ISOs)
ownerCompensationOptions: Fringe benefits (deductible to corp, tax-free to employee)
advantages: Fringe benefits (health, disability, life) deductible and tax-free to owner
disadvantages: 21% corporate rate on all profits (even if not needed personally)

delawareCCorp.note: Delaware C corps pay federal 21% corporate tax. No Delaware income tax on out-of-state income.

authorizedSharesMethod.note: Default method — Delaware bills this amount unless you request the Assumed Par Value method. Most startups with millions of authorized shares OVERPAY using this method.

requiredInputs: total gross assets from Form 1120 Schedule L
assumedParValueMethod.note: Almost always lower for startups with many authorized shares but modest assets. Must request this method explicitly. $400/million of gross assets, minimum $400.
assumedParValueMethod.example: Company with $2M gross assets and 10M authorized shares: $400 x 2 = $800 tax vs. $85,000+ under Authorized Shares Method.

quarterlyEstimatedPayments.note: Corporations owing $5,000+ must pay estimated taxes quarterly.

franchiseTax.taxSavingsTip: Always calculate using BOTH methods and submit the lower amount. The Assumed Par Value Method typically saves startups with many authorized shares $10,000–$80,000+ per year vs. the default Authorized Shares Method.
franchiseTax._correctionNote: Confirmed June 2026 directly from corp.delaware.gov. Previous entry had $175 minimum only — now includes complete fee schedule, Assumed Par Value minimum ($400), filing fee ($50), late penalty ($200 + 1.5%/mo), Large Corporate Filer cap ($250,000).

delawareCCorp.bestFor: Raising VC funding, IPO path, companies with many employees needing stock options
delawareCCorp.notBestFor: Solo bootstrapped founders, small businesses, privacy-conscious founders

wyomingLLC.note: Wyoming does not require member names to be public. Strong charging order protection.
wyomingLLC.bestFor: Privacy, low cost, bootstrapped founders wanting C corp or LLC with no franchise tax
wyomingLLC.foreignQualificationNote: If operating in another state, must foreign qualify and pay that state's fees and taxes too.

wyomingCCorp.note: Most VCs require Delaware C Corp. Wyoming C Corp is not standard for institutional investors.
wyomingCCorp.bestFor: Bootstrapped founders wanting C corp structure with max privacy and minimum cost

professionalLLC.note: Required in some states for licensed professionals (doctors, lawyers, CPAs, engineers). Same tax treatment as regular LLC.
professionalLLC.states: Requirements vary by state and profession

husbandWifeQualifiedJointVenture.taxForm: Two separate Schedule Cs (one per spouse)
husbandWifeQualifiedJointVenture.note: Married couples operating a business together can elect QJV status to avoid filing Form 1065 as a partnership. Each spouse files their own Schedule C for their ownership share. Both build individual Social Security credits. No K-1 required.
requirements: Both must materially participate in the business
Business must NOT be incorporated or organized as a state-registered LLC (see llcRestriction below)
Election is made by filing two separate Schedule Cs — no formal IRS form required

llcRestriction.rule: If the couple has formed a state-registered LLC, QJV status is ONLY available if they reside in a community property state. In all other states, a spousal LLC must file as a partnership (Form 1065) or elect S corp / C corp status.
llcRestriction.communityPropertyNote: In community property states, a husband-wife LLC that is wholly owned by the spouses may be treated as a disregarded entity (two-owner QJV) for federal tax purposes under Rev. Proc. 2002-69. This is a special IRS accommodation for community property law states only.
llcRestriction.nonCommunityPropertyWarning: In the 41 non-community-property states (and DC), a registered husband-wife LLC cannot use QJV treatment. The LLC must file Form 1065 as a multi-member LLC partnership, OR elect to be taxed as an S corp (Form 2553) or C corp. Attempting QJV on a registered LLC in a non-CP state is a filing error.
llcRestriction.unregisteredBusinessNote: If the couple operates as a general partnership WITHOUT forming a state-registered LLC or corporation, QJV election is available in ALL 50 states regardless of community property status.

stateOverlayValidation.trigger: When user selects husbandWifeQualifiedJointVenture AND has indicated they formed an LLC

ifCommunityPropertyState.uiMessage: Because you are in a community property state, your husband-wife LLC may qualify for QJV treatment under Rev. Proc. 2002-69. Each spouse files a separate Schedule C.

ifNonCommunityPropertyState.uiMessage: Warning: You have indicated you formed an LLC. In {state}, which is not a community property state, a husband-wife LLC cannot use Qualified Joint Venture treatment. Your LLC must file as a partnership (Form 1065) or elect S Corp status. Consider consulting a CPA.

ifNoLLCFormed.uiMessage: Because your business is not a registered LLC or corporation, you can elect QJV treatment in any state. Each spouse reports their share on a separate Schedule C.

husbandWifeQualifiedJointVenture.socialSecurityBenefit: Each spouse earns individual Social Security credits on their respective Schedule C income. This is a significant long-term retirement planning advantage over a single-owner Schedule C where only one spouse builds credits.
husbandWifeQualifiedJointVenture.incomeAllocationNote: Spouses allocate income and deductions based on their actual ownership percentage or participation share. The split does not have to be 50/50 but must reflect actual contribution. Document the agreed allocation in writing.

partnershipAdvantages: Required if LLC registered in non-CP state

generalPartnership.note: Each general partner pays SE tax on their distributive share. Unlimited liability — rarely advisable without LLC protection.

sCorpCCorp.taxForm: Form 1120 then Form 1120-S (if S election after C corp formation)
sCorpCCorp.note: S Corp election filed after C corp incorporation. Most commonly used via LLC + Form 2553 today.

scheduleE.note: Used for rental income where NO substantial services are provided to tenants. Passive activity loss rules limit ability to deduct losses against non-passive income unless real estate professional.

scheduleF.note: Farm income and expenses. Different deduction rules from Schedule C. Unique items: livestock, crop insurance, soil conservation, farm equipment depreciation.

clergy.housingAllowance: Tax-free if designated in advance by employing church. Excluded from income but included in SE tax base.
clergy.canOptOut: Form 4361 — irrevocable opt-out from SE tax on ministerial earnings if opposed on religious grounds
clergy.note: Ministers may be employees for income tax but self-employed for SE tax. Complex dual status.

householdEmployer.note: If you pay a nanny, housekeeper, or household worker $2,800+ in 2026, you are a household employer. You owe employer FICA + may need to withhold employee FICA and federal income tax. Nanny Tax.

limitedPartnership.seTaxApplies: General partners only. Limited partners generally NOT subject to SE tax on their distributive share.
limitedPartnership.structure: 1+ general partners (unlimited liability, manage business) + 1+ limited partners (liability limited to investment, passive role)
limitedPartnership.bestFor: Real estate investment funds, private equity, film production partnerships
limitedPartnership.keyTaxPoint: Limited partner K-1 income goes on Schedule E (passive) — no SE tax. General partner income goes on Schedule E but IS subject to SE tax.
limitedPartnership.note: Limited partners who actively participate in management risk being reclassified as general partners by IRS.

professionalCorporation.taxForm: Form 1120 (C corp election) or Form 1120-S (S corp election)
professionalCorporation.stateRequirement: Required in many states for licensed professionals: doctors, dentists, attorneys, CPAs, architects, engineers
professionalCorporation.personalHoldingCompanyTax: PC taxed as C corp faces potential personal holding company tax (20%) if too much passive income retained
professionalCorporation.bestFor: Licensed professionals who need liability protection and want employee benefits structure
professionalCorporation.corporateTaxNote: PC can elect S corp status if shareholders are eligible. Most medical/dental PCs use S corp election.

familyLLC.taxForm: Form 1065 (partnership) or Schedule C if disregarded
familyLLC.seTaxApplies: Managing member typically. Limited member children/family: generally no SE tax on passive distributions.
familyLLC.purpose: Estate planning tool. Transfer ownership interests to family members at reduced valuations (valuation discounts for lack of control/marketability).
familyLLC.incomeShifting: Distributions to lower-bracket family members taxed at their lower rate. Kiddie tax (unearned income of children) applies to children under 19 (or full-time students under 24).
familyLLC.kiddie\u7a0eNote: Kiddie tax: child's unearned income (including LLC distributions) taxed at parents' rate if under age 19. Calculator must model this.
familyLLC.note: Must have legitimate business purpose beyond tax avoidance. Charging order protection for creditors.

holdingCompany.taxForm: Depends on entity type (LLC = Schedule C/1065; Corp = 1120)
holdingCompany.structure: Parent entity owns one or more subsidiary/operating companies

commonStructures.name: Wyoming LLC Holding + Operating LLC
commonStructures.note: Privacy + liability protection. No franchise tax.

commonStructures.name: Delaware C Corp Holding + Operating LLCs
commonStructures.note: Standard for VC-backed companies with multiple product lines.

commonStructures.name: Management Company + Operating Company
commonStructures.note: Mgmt company bills operating company for services. Common tax planning structure.

holdingCompany.managementCompanyNote: Management company (owned by operators) charges operating company a management fee. Operating company deducts fee. Management company income distributed to owners. SE tax applies on management fee income.
seoKeywords: holding company vs operating company taxes

realEstateHoldingLLC.taxForm: Schedule E (single property, SMLLC disregarded) or Form 1065 (partnership)
realEstateHoldingLLC.purpose: Hold real estate assets. Separate entity per property recommended for liability isolation.
realEstateHoldingLLC.keyBenefit: Liability shield: lawsuit from one property cannot reach assets in other LLCs or personal assets if properly maintained.
realEstateHoldingLLC.mortgageNote: Due-on-sale clause: some lenders accelerate loan if property transferred to LLC. Consult attorney. Portfolio lenders more flexible.
realEstateHoldingLLC.deductions: Same as landlord Schedule E: depreciation, mortgage interest, taxes, insurance, maintenance
realEstateHoldingLLC.series_llc_alternative: Some states allow series LLC — one LLC with segregated 'series' for each property. Not recognized in all states.
seoKeywords: LLC for rental property tax benefits

seriesLLC.taxForm: Varies by state. IRS has not issued definitive guidance on series LLC federal tax treatment.
seriesLLC.availability: Available in: Delaware, Texas, Illinois, Nevada, Utah, Iowa, Kansas, Missouri, Montana, North Dakota, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Virginia, Wisconsin
seriesLLC.concept: One master LLC with 'series' (cells) that are legally separate from each other. Each series can own property, have members, and have separate liability.
seriesLLC.irsUncertainty: IRS has not fully clarified whether each series is a separate tax entity. Draft regulations pending since 2010. Most practitioners treat each series as separate entity for tax purposes.
seriesLLC.risk: Not recognized in all states. Courts in non-series states may not honor inter-series liability protection.
seriesLLC.bestFor: Real estate investors in series-friendly states who want multiple properties with liability isolation but only one annual filing.
seoKeywords: series LLC vs multiple LLCs taxes

statutoryEmployee.definition: Worker who is not a common-law employee but is treated as an employee for FICA purposes by statute. Receives W-2 with Box 13 'Statutory Employee' checked.
categories: Drivers who distribute food/beverages, pick up/deliver laundry, or distribute meat/produce
Individuals who work at home on materials supplied by employer

taxTreatment.ficaWithheld: Yes — on W-2 like regular employees
taxTreatment.incomeTaxWithheld: No — unless agreed to by worker
taxTreatment.businessDeductions: Can deduct business expenses on Schedule C against statutory employee income (Box 1 of W-2)
taxTreatment.netResult: Report income + deductions on Schedule C but SE tax already handled via FICA

seoKeywords: statutory employee vs independent contractor taxes

w2Employee.ficaSplit: Employer pays 50%, employee pays 50% (6.2% SS + 1.45% Medicare each)
w2Employee.quarterlyEstimated: Generally not required if withholding sufficient

1099NEC.note: OBBBA raised threshold from $600 to $2,000 effective 2026. Income still taxable even without 1099-NEC below threshold.

1099K.note: Threshold restored to pre-2021 level by OBBBA. Income taxable even if 1099-K not issued. Gross receipts shown — must subtract fees, refunds, COGS to get taxable amount.

1099MISC.note: Used for rents, prizes, awards, and certain other payments. Not for services (that's 1099-NEC).

taxability.formula: Combined income = AGI (excl SS) + tax-exempt interest + 0.5 x annual SSDI benefit

mfs_living_together.note: 85% taxable at any income. No threshold benefit.

taxability.ssiNote: SSI (Supplemental Security Income) is NEVER taxable. Completely different program from SSDI.

ssdi.note: SGA = Substantial Gainful Activity. Earning above SGA consistently may trigger SSDI eligibility review.
ssdi._sgaCorrectionNote: Corrected June 2026: previous values ($1,620/$2,700) were 2025 SGA limits. 2026 SGA limits per SSA (2.8% COLA applied): $1,690/mo non-blind, $2,830/mo blind. Source: SSA.gov, Atticus, DisabilityExchange.org, Michael Armstrong Law.

taxability.employerPaid100pct: 100% taxable as ordinary income. W-2 issued.
taxability.individualPolicy: 100% tax-free if premiums paid with after-tax dollars.

shortTermDisability.note: No SE tax on disability benefits regardless of taxability.

longTermDisability.typicalBenefit: 60% of pre-disability salary (gross) or 60–70% of after-tax salary (individual policies)
longTermDisability.taxability: Same rules as STD. Employer-paid = taxable. Employee after-tax = tax-free.
longTermDisability.ssdiBenefitOffset: Most employer LTD policies offset benefits by SSDI amount received. Net LTD check = gross LTD - SSDI.
longTermDisability.taxOnOffsetNote: IRS taxes full gross LTD benefit (before SSDI offset reduction). You pay taxes on money your insurer kept.
longTermDisability.openEnrollmentTip: Electing to pay LTD premiums with after-tax dollars makes all future benefits tax-free. Costs slightly more now, saves significantly if disability occurs.

rentalIncome.scheduleC_if: You provide substantial services (hotels, B&Bs, short-term with daily cleaning/meals)
rentalIncome.augustaRuleExclusion: Renting personal home 14 days or fewer — income fully excluded from gross income
rentalIncome.vacancyVsOccupancy: Mixed-use (personal + rental) requires prorating deductions by days used
rentalIncome.realEstateProfessional: 750+ hours/year in real estate and more hours in RE than any other profession — losses become non-passive, deductible against all income.

kIncome.seTaxApplies: General partners and LLC members: SE tax on their distributive share. Limited partners and S corp shareholders: generally no SE tax on K-1 distributions (wages separate).
kIncome.note: K-1 income is not subject to withholding. Quarterly estimated taxes typically required.

cryptoIncome.miningIncome: Schedule C — ordinary income + SE tax at fair market value on date received
cryptoIncome.stakingIncome: Treated as ordinary income when received per IRS Rev. Rul. 2023-14
cryptoIncome.cryptoSales: Capital gain/loss. Short-term if held <1 year, long-term if >1 year
cryptoIncome.nftSales: Short-term or long-term capital gain. Creator NFT royalties = ordinary income
cryptoIncome.form: Form 8949 + Schedule D for sales. Schedule C for mining/staking income.

acaSubsidy.cliffNote: OBBBA did NOT extend enhanced subsidies past Dec 31, 2025. Hard 400% FPL cliff returned January 1, 2026.
acaSubsidy.medicaidExpansionStates: 138% FPL lower bound in states that expanded Medicaid (38 states + DC as of 2026)

fpl2026.note: Based on 2025 HHS poverty guidelines used for 2026 coverage. Alaska and Hawaii higher.

cliffThresholds400pct.note: Earning $1 above these amounts = $0 premium tax credit. Plan income carefully.

acaSubsidy.magi_definition: AGI + tax-exempt interest + nontaxable Social Security + foreign earned income exclusion
acaSubsidy.benchmark: Second Lowest-Cost Silver Plan (SLCSP) in enrollee's rating area
acaSubsidy.subsidy_formula: Monthly subsidy = SLCSP premium - (MAGI x applicable_pct / 12)

clawback.below400pct: Partial clawback if income underestimated. Capped based on FPL bracket.
clawback.above400pct: Full repayment of all APTC received. No cap.

incomeReducingStrategies: Self-employed health insurance deduction (circular interaction — use Pub 974 method)
Business deductions on Schedule C reduce net SE income → AGI → MAGI
Avoid Roth conversions in subsidy-eligible years
Delay taxable account capital gains realization
Use DAF for charitable giving if itemizing
acaSubsidy.csrNote: Cost-Sharing Reductions available at 100–250% FPL on Silver plans. Reduce deductibles and out-of-pocket costs.

instacart.note: Full-service shopper = SE. In-store shopper = W-2.

thumbtack.note: Lead fees are 100% deductible business expense.

babysitter_nanny_1099.note: Critical distinction: nanny working regular hours in employer's home = household employee (W-2, Schedule H). Independent babysitter working for multiple families = 1099 contractor. Calculator must route based on work arrangement.
babysitter_nanny_1099.ficaNote: If nanny earns $2,800+ from single employer in 2026, employer owes FICA. File Schedule H with Form 1040.

ugc_creator.description: Creates content for brands to use on brand's channels. Does not post on own audience.
ugc_creator.note: Rising category. Paid per deliverable, not per follower. Brand pays direct or via UGC marketplace (Billo, Insense, JoinBrands).

online_course_creator.note: Udemy courses: revenue share varies (37%–97% depending on source). Kajabi: flat monthly fee, no revenue share. Platform fee structure matters for deduction calculation.

newsletter_business.note: Beehiiv charges flat fee + % on paid subscriptions. Sponsorship income on 1099-NEC above $2,000 threshold.

sponsorship_income.note: Any creator receiving paid brand sponsorships. Brand issues 1099-NEC if $2,000+ paid in year. Below threshold: still taxable, just no form issued.
taxableItems: Gifted products at fair market value (FMV)
sponsorship_income.giftedProductsNote: Gifted products from brands are taxable income at FMV even if never sold. Common mistake: creators don't report PR packages.

brand_deal_calculator.description: Standalone one-page tool: user enters single brand deal amount, sees instant tax breakdown.
brand_deal_calculator.targetUser: Creator who just got a $5,000 or $10,000 brand deal and wants to know what to set aside.
keyOutputs: Federal income tax estimate at their bracket
brand_deal_calculator.viralityNote: Most shareable standalone tool on the site. Creators post these results on social media.
seoKeywords: how much tax on $10000 brand deal

criticalDistinction.personalItems_soldAtLoss: Not taxable. Loss on personal property is not deductible.
criticalDistinction.personalItems_soldAtGain: Taxable as capital gain. Short-term if owned <1 year.
criticalDistinction.inventoryForResale: Schedule C — ordinary income + SE tax.
criticalDistinction.routingQuestion: Calculator must ask: 'Are you reselling for profit (business) or selling personal belongings?'

facebook_marketplace.note: Facebook Marketplace introduced selling fees in some categories. Payments via Facebook Pay tracked for 1099-K.

stockx_goat.note: Sneaker and streetwear resale. StockX charges seller fee (~8–9.5% depending on seller level) + transaction fee. GOAT charges commission 15–25%.
stockx_goat.keyTaxQuestion: Business vs investment activity. Frequent flippers = ordinary income (Schedule C). Occasional sellers = capital gain (Schedule D).

taxTreatment.businessReseller: Schedule C — ordinary income + SE tax. Can deduct COGS, platform fees, authentication fees, shipping.
taxTreatment.investmentActivity: Schedule D — short or long-term capital gain. Cost basis = purchase price + shipping + fees.

printful_printify.description: Print-on-demand: designs made by seller, products fulfilled by Printful/Printify. No inventory held.
printful_printify.cogsNote: COGS = base cost of each item fulfilled by Printful/Printify. This is the biggest and most important deduction for POD sellers.
printful_printify.platformFee: None — POD supplier charges base product price. Seller sets retail price above base cost.
printful_printify.note: High volume POD sellers: COGS tracking is essential. Printful/Printify monthly statements show total product cost — this is your COGS.

airbnb.scheduleUsed: Schedule C if substantial services; Schedule E if simple rent
airbnb.note: Airbnb takes approx 3% host fee. Guest pays Airbnb fee separately.

turo.note: Cannot use standard mileage if previously used MACRS depreciation on vehicle.

getaround.description: Peer-to-peer car sharing. Getaround takes 40% of each trip. Owner keeps 60%.
getaround.note: Getaround fee already netted from payment. Deduct actual vehicle expenses at business-use percentage.

rv_rental.depreciationNote: RVs depreciate over 5 years (MACRS) for business use. Section 179 may apply if >50% business use.

boat_rental.scheduleUsed: Schedule C (with captain services) or Schedule E (bare boat charter)
boat_rental.note: Bare boat charter (no captain) = more likely Schedule E. Charter with captain providing services = Schedule C.

parking_space_rental.note: Passive income — generally no SE tax. Losses subject to passive activity rules.

storage_rental.note: Renting storage space in home, garage, or outbuilding. Generally Schedule E passive income.

short_vs_long_term_rental.description: Comparison calculator: same property modeled as STR vs LTR

shortTermRental.scheduleUsed: Schedule C if substantial services; Schedule E if simple
shortTermRental.seTaxRisk: If Schedule C applies, SE tax on net profit
shortTermRental.deductions: All STR expenses prorated by days rented

short_vs_long_term_rental.keystoneQuestion: 7-day average rental period test: average rental ≤7 days = not passive (Schedule C rules). >7 days average = passive (Schedule E).
seoKeywords: short term vs long term rental tax comparison
Airbnb vs long term rental taxes

passiveLossRule.realEstateProfessionalException: 750+ hours AND more hours in RE than any other activity — losses become non-passive

landlord.depreciationNote: Residential property depreciates over 27.5 years. Land is NOT depreciable. Cost segregation study can accelerate depreciation for commercial.
landlord.note1031Exchange: Like-kind exchange: defer capital gain taxes by reinvesting sale proceeds into a new property within 45/180 day deadlines.
seoKeywords: how much tax on rental income

real_estate_agent_rental.note: Real estate agents who also own rental properties have a unique advantage: if they qualify as real estate professionals (750+ hours, majority of work time in RE), passive loss rules don't apply — rental losses can offset all income.
real_estate_agent_rental.scheduleUsed: Schedule C for agent commissions. Schedule E for rental income.
seoKeywords: real estate agent rental income taxes

CA.name: California
CA.note: CA SDI applies to SE income. 9 income tax brackets. Highest top rate in US.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.note: Rate was 5.39%, reduced to 5.19% mid-2025. Further phasedowns planned.

IN.name: Indiana
IN.note: Rate has been decreasing. Some counties add local income tax.

MD.name: Maryland
MD.note: New 6.25% and 6.5% high-income brackets added in 2025 BRF Act, effective retroactively.

MS.name: Mississippi
MS.note: Phasing down toward 0% if revenue triggers met.

NE.name: Nebraska
NE.note: Top rate reduced from 5.2% to 4.55% in 2026. Further reductions to 3.99% by 2027.

NH.name: New Hampshire
NH.note: Dividend/interest tax fully eliminated effective Jan 1, 2025.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.note: Final reduction in phased plan. Rate was 4.25% in 2025.

OH.name: Ohio
OH.note: Converted to flat tax in 2026. Income at or below $26,050 is exempt.

OK.name: Oklahoma
OK.note: Simplified to 3 brackets in 2026, top rate reduced from 4.75% to 4.5%.

WA.name: Washington
WA.note: No general income tax. Capital gains tax 7% on gains >$262,000 (2026), 9.9% on gains >$1M.

mfs.label: Married Filing Separately
mfs.note: Rarely beneficial. Ineligible for QBI, EITC, education credits, ACA subsidy, most deductions.

hoh.label: Head of Household
hoh.note: Must be unmarried, paid >50% of household costs, and have qualifying person.

qsw.label: Qualifying Surviving Spouse
qsw.note: Use for 2 years after spouse's death if dependent child.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: W-2 job + gig side income
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: W-2 withholding may not cover SE tax on gig income. Check if quarterly payments needed. Gap = SE tax on net gig profit.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: W-2 job + Etsy/seller income
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Same as above. COGS and platform fees reduce taxable seller income.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: W-2 job + 1099 consulting
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: SE tax owed on 1099 net income. May be able to use W-2 withholding to cover via extra withholding (W-4 line 4c).

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: W-2 spouse + self-employed spouse
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Combined income may push into higher brackets. SE spouse pays SE tax. W-2 spouse withholding may be insufficient.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: Two 1099 spouses
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Both pay SE tax. Both need quarterly payments. Joint filing usually better than MFS.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: S Corp owner + W-2 spouse
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: S Corp salary + distributions. Spouse W-2 withholding separate. Model joint vs separate comparison.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: SSDI + gig income
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Dual complexity: SE tax on gig income + SSDI taxability from combined income formula + SGA limit monitoring.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: SSDI + W-2 spouse income
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Spouse W-2 may push combined income above $44K MFJ threshold, making up to 85% of SSDI taxable.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: LTD benefits + part-time W-2
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: LTD taxability + W-2 withholding. Check if LTD payer withheld enough.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: Retiree (Social Security) + gig
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: SS combined income formula applies. Gig income can trigger SS taxation.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: Student + gig income
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Gig income creates SE tax obligation. Parents' return may still claim student as dependent if dependency test met.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: W-2 + Airbnb/rental income
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Rental usually Schedule E (passive). Losses limited unless real estate professional.

mixedHouseholdScenarios.label: Parent hiring child in business
mixedHouseholdScenarios.note: Sole prop/SMLLC: child wages exempt from FICA under 18. Real work, fair wages, documentation required.

bookkeeper_accountant.note: CPAs and EAs (Enrolled Agents) have additional licensing fees deductible as professional expenses.

virtual_assistant.note: VA services are highly deductible-friendly — almost all expenses are home-office and tech based. High S Corp potential at $80K+.

coach.note: Coaching certification costs are deductible as professional development (must be maintaining/improving current skills, not qualifying for new career).

insurance_agent.note: Captive agents may be W-2. Independent agents are typically 1099. Commissions are SE income.

notary_signing_agent.note: Mobile notary and loan signing agents: mileage between appointments is the largest deduction. Track every signing trip.

tutor.note: Online tutors: home office is key deduction. In-person tutors: mileage between student locations is #1 deduction.

wedding_event_planner.note: Deposits collected on behalf of clients are not income until earned. Vendor payments passed through to client are not deductible if reimbursed.

qa_tester_contractor.note: Common in tech as contract workers. Devices used for testing (phones, tablets) qualify for Section 179 deduction at business-use percentage.

housingAllowance.taxTreatment: Excludable from federal income tax if designated in advance by church. Amount limited to: lesser of (a) designated amount, (b) actual housing expenses, (c) fair rental value of home furnished + utilities.
housingAllowance.seTaxNote: Housing allowance IS included in SE tax base even though excluded from income tax. This surprises many ministers.
housingAllowance.form: No separate form — designated in church board minutes before year begins

seOptOut.note: Irrevocable election to opt out of SE tax on ministerial earnings if conscientiously opposed on religious grounds. Cannot opt back in.
seOptOut.deadline: Due date of return for second year with net ministerial earnings of $400+

uniqueRules.dualStatus: Ministers are employees for income tax purposes but self-employed for SE tax on ministerial income. Unique in US tax code.

uniqueDeductions.name: Feed
uniqueDeductions.note: Livestock feed purchased and consumed in tax year. Cannot deduct feed purchased but not yet used (inventory rules apply for some farms).

uniqueDeductions.name: Seeds and plants
uniqueDeductions.note: Purchased for planting, not for consumption. Ornamental seeds for personal use are not deductible.

uniqueDeductions.name: Fertilizers and lime
uniqueDeductions.note: Soil conditioners and crop nutrients applied in the tax year. Purchased but unused inventory may need to be deducted in future year.

uniqueDeductions.name: Livestock purchased for resale
uniqueDeductions.note: Cost of animals purchased during the year and sold in the same year. Treated as COGS on Schedule F. Livestock raised on farm has $0 basis.

uniqueDeductions.name: Breeding livestock depreciation
uniqueDeductions.note: Breeding animals are depreciable assets (not expensed as feed). Cattle and horses: 5–7 years MACRS. Section 179 or bonus depreciation may apply.

uniqueDeductions.name: Farm equipment
uniqueDeductions.note: Tractors, combines, irrigation systems, planting/harvesting equipment. Section 179 up to $1,220,000 (2026). 100% bonus depreciation available under OBBBA.

uniqueDeductions.name: Crop insurance proceeds deferral
uniqueDeductions.note: Crop insurance is income, not a deduction. However, farmers can elect to defer crop insurance proceeds to the following tax year if the crop would normally be sold in a different year. Elective deferral, not automatic.

uniqueDeductions.name: Fuel for farm equipment
uniqueDeductions.note: 100% deductible for off-highway business use. Also qualifies for federal fuel tax credit (Form 4136) — separate from the deduction.

uniqueDeductions.name: Repairs and maintenance of farm buildings
uniqueDeductions.note: Routine repairs are an operating expense. Improvements that add value or extend useful life must be capitalized and depreciated. Tangible property regulations (Repair Regs) govern the distinction.

specialProvisions.farmingLossCarryback: Net farming losses can be carried back 2 years (vs 0 years for most businesses).
specialProvisions.incomeAveraging: Schedule J — farmers can average income over 3 years to reduce tax on boom years.
specialProvisions.soilConservation: Deductible up to 25% of gross farming income
specialProvisions.fuelTaxCredit: Form 4136 — credit for non-highway fuel used in farming operations

farm_schedule_f._deductionSchema: name:string, deductible:number (0–1 or dollar cap), note:string — matches selfEmploymentDeductions.otherCommonDeductions schema

staysSoleProprietor.reason: S Corp compliance cost ($1,500–$4,000) exceeds FICA savings at this level.

considerSCorp.reason: FICA savings on distributions begin to outpace compliance costs.

strongSCorp.reason: Clear positive ROI. Savings typically $5,000–$15,000+ vs sole prop.

considerCCorp.reason: 21% corporate rate on retained earnings may beat personal rate. Benefits structure.

delawareCCorp.trigger: Raising VC or angel investment. Need startup-standard equity structure.

wyomingLLC.trigger: Want C Corp or LLC structure, no investors, maximum privacy, lowest cost.

entityRecommender.scorp_election_timing: File Form 2553 by March 15 for current calendar year. Or file within 75 days of formation for new entities.
entityRecommender.california_note: California S Corps pay minimum franchise tax of $800/year even if no profit. Changes S Corp breakeven point upward in CA.

importantForms.1040X: Amended US Individual Income Tax Return
importantForms.1120: US Corporation Income Tax Return (C Corp)
importantForms.1120S: US Income Tax Return for an S Corporation
importantForms.K1_1065: Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) — Partner's Share of Income
importantForms.ScheduleC: Profit or Loss from Business (Sole Proprietorship)
importantForms.ScheduleE: Supplemental Income and Loss (Rental, K-1, etc.)
importantForms.2553: Election by a Small Business Corporation (S Corp election)
importantForms.8829: Expenses for Business Use of Your Home
importantForms.8949: Sales and Other Dispositions of Capital Assets
importantForms.1099K: Payment Card and Third Party Network Transactions ($20,000/200 transactions threshold 2026)
importantForms.4361: Application for Exemption from SE Tax for Clergy

primaryKeywords: LLC vs S Corp tax calculator
how much to set aside for taxes 1099
W-2 plus RSU combined tax calculator

legalDisclaimer: This configuration file is for educational and calculator tool purposes only. Tax laws change frequently. Always verify current rates with IRS.gov, SSA.gov, or a qualified CPA or tax attorney before making financial decisions. Numbers reflect best available information as of June 2026 based on IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-32, IRS Notice 2025-67, IRS Notice IR-2025-128, HHS 2026 FPL guidelines, and OBBBA provisions signed July 4, 2025.

stateMetadata.communityPropertyNote: In community property states, income earned during marriage is generally owned equally by both spouses. This affects QJV eligibility, MFS filing strategies, and retirement account contribution rules.

MD: All counties require local tax filing (2.25%–3.20% depending on county)
OH: Many municipalities levy local income tax (0%–3%). RITA and CCA administer most.
PA: Many municipalities levy earned income tax. Philadelphia: 3.75% resident.
IN: All 92 counties levy local income tax (0.50%–2.90%).

CA.note: CA SDI applies to self-employed who voluntarily elect coverage. Mandatory for W-2 employees.

NJ.note: NJ SDI mandatory for employees. SE may not be subject.

NY.note: NY DBL (statutory disability) mandatory for employees. SE not required.

passthroughEntityTaxStates.note: Many states now allow pass-through entities (partnerships, S corps) to pay state income tax at the entity level, providing a workaround to the federal SALT cap. Known as PTET or SALT workaround.
passthroughEntityTaxStates.howItWorks: Entity pays state tax on behalf of owners. Owners get state tax credit on their personal return. The entity-level payment is deductible as a business expense, bypassing the $40,400 SALT cap.

equityCompensation._schema: All equity objects share: incomeEvent, taxEvent, form1099B_basisTrap, quarterlyNote
equityCompensation._overview: Equity compensation creates two taxable events: (1) ordinary income when shares vest or options are exercised, (2) capital gain/loss when shares are sold. Misunderstanding this two-layer structure is the most expensive equity tax mistake.

rsu.label: Restricted Stock Units

taxEvent1_vesting.amountTaxable: FMV on vesting date × shares vested
taxEvent1_vesting.withholdingRisk: 22% under-withholds for anyone in 32%+ bracket. Gap = (marginal_rate - 0.22) x RSU_income. Fix via increased W-4 withholding or quarterly payments.

taxEvent2_sale.costBasis: FMV at vesting (already reported as W-2 income)
taxEvent2_sale.shortTerm: Sold within 1 year of vesting → ordinary income rates
taxEvent2_sale.longTerm: Sold more than 1 year after vesting → LTCG rates 0/15/20%
taxEvent2_sale.form1099B_basisTrap: Broker may report $0 or purchase price instead of FMV at vesting. Adjust on Form 8949 to avoid double taxation.

rsu.quarterlyRequired: If withholding gap > $1,000 after W-4 optimization

iso.label: Incentive Stock Options

amtAtExercise.bargainElement: FMV at exercise minus strike price = AMT preference item
amtAtExercise.amtTrap: Large ISO exercise in single year can trigger AMT even without selling shares. Strategy: exercise in tranches across multiple years to stay below AMT exemption phaseout.
amtAtExercise.amtCreditCarryforward: AMT paid generates Form 8801 credit to offset future regular tax

qualifyingDisposition.taxTreatment: Entire gain (sale price minus strike price) as LONG-TERM capital gain
qualifyingDisposition.californiaWarning: California taxes ISO qualifying dispositions at ordinary income state rates — no preferential CA treatment

disqualifyingDisposition.ordinaryIncome: Bargain element at exercise — added to W-2
disqualifyingDisposition.capitalGain: Sale price minus FMV at exercise — STCG or LTCG depending on holding from exercise to sale

annualLimit_isoVsNso.note: Aggregate FMV at grant date exceeding $100,000 exercisable in one year — excess treated as NSOs

iso.quarterlyRequired: If projected AMT liability exceeds withholding by more than $1,000

nso.label: Non-Qualified Stock Options

taxEvent1_exercise.spread: FMV at exercise minus strike price
taxEvent1_exercise.withholding_contractor: Must pay via quarterly estimated taxes
taxEvent1_exercise.cashNeeded: Tax owed at exercise even if shares not sold. Cashless same-day sale covers obligation but triggers immediate sale.

taxEvent2_sale.longTerm: Sold more than 1 year after exercise
taxEvent2_sale.basisTrap: 1099-B often shows wrong basis — adjust on Form 8949

nso.quarterlyRequired: Contractors always. Employees if withholding insufficient.

espp.label: Employee Stock Purchase Plan (Section 423 Qualified)
espp.ficaNote: Section 423 qualified ESPP shares: NO FICA on either qualifying or disqualifying dispositions. Non-qualified ESPPs ARE subject to FICA at purchase.

qualifyingDispositionTax.ordinaryIncome: LESSER of: (a) 15% x FMV at offering date, or (b) actual gain (sale price minus purchase price)
qualifyingDispositionTax.ltcg: Remaining gain above ordinary income portion
qualifyingDispositionTax.withholdingNote: Qualifying disposition ordinary income NOT subject to federal withholding. Must pay via estimated taxes or W-4 adjustment.
qualifyingDispositionTax.basisAdjusted: Purchase price + ordinary income per share

disqualifyingDispositionTax.ordinaryIncome: FMV at purchase date minus purchase price (full spread on purchase date)
disqualifyingDispositionTax.capitalGain: Sale price minus FMV at purchase date
disqualifyingDispositionTax.capitalGainType: STCG if held under 1 year from purchase; LTCG if over 1 year
disqualifyingDispositionTax.form: W-2 (employer adds ordinary income); 1099-B for proceeds

basisTrap.description: Broker 1099-B reports purchase price as basis — not adjusted basis. Adjusted basis = purchase price + ordinary income already on W-2. Failing to adjust = paying tax twice.
basisTrap.form8949Code: Adjust Column E with correct adjusted basis

espp.form3922: Employer files Form 3922 at transfer. Use to verify offering date FMV, purchase date FMV, and purchase price.
espp.quarterlyRequired: For qualifying dispositions where ordinary income not withheld

qsbs.label: Qualified Small Business Stock (Section 1202)
qsbs.overview: Founders and early investors in qualifying C corps can exclude up to 100% of capital gains. Most powerful wealth-building provision in the tax code for startup equity.
qsbs.exclusionCapCalc: Greater of ($15M post-July-2025 stock / $10M pre-July-2025 stock) OR (10 x adjusted basis). Use higher.
qsbs.amtTreatment: Post-Sept 27, 2010 QSBS: no AMT preference. Full exclusion from AMT, NIIT, and regular capital gains tax.
qsbs.niitTreatment: Excluded gain NOT subject to 3.8% NIIT.
qualifyingRequirements: Original issuance from domestic C corporation
Gross assets under $50M at time of issuance
Qualifying trade or business (excludes professional services, finance, hospitality, farming, mining)
Acquired for cash, property, or services — not purchased from existing shareholders

stateConformity.note: California taxes QSBS gains at full state rate (up to 13.3%) even when 100% excluded federally.

qsbs.qsbsStacking: Gifting QSBS to family members before sale multiplies the per-person $15M exclusion cap. Each recipient (spouse, children, trusts) gets own cap.
qsbs.section1045Rollover: If QSBS sold before holding period: defer gain by reinvesting in new QSBS within 60 days.
qsbs.quarterlyRequired: State estimated tax in non-conforming states on excluded gain. Federal: no quarterly on excluded portion.

phantomStock_SAR.label: Phantom Stock and Stock Appreciation Rights
phantomStock_SAR.taxTreatment: Ordinary income when paid or settled — no LTCG treatment
phantomStock_SAR.note: Common in S corps (which cannot have two stock classes) and LLCs. Tax treatment mirrors a cash bonus.

form1099B_basisReconciliation._purpose: The #1 equity tax filing error: paying tax twice due to under-reported cost basis on Form 1099-B
form1099B_basisReconciliation.problem: Broker 1099-B shows $0 or purchase price as basis — not the adjusted basis that includes income already on W-2
form1099B_basisReconciliation.rsuAdjustment: Correct basis = FMV at vesting date. If 1099-B shows $0, enter FMV at vesting in Form 8949 Column E.
form1099B_basisReconciliation.esppQualifyingAdjustment: Correct basis = purchase price + ordinary income per share already on W-2
form1099B_basisReconciliation.esppDisqualifyingAdjustment: Correct basis = FMV at purchase date
form1099B_basisReconciliation.isoDisqualifyingAdjustment: Correct basis = FMV at exercise date (spread already taxed as W-2)
form1099B_basisReconciliation.form8949Codes: Code B (STCG with 1099-B) or Code E (LTCG with 1099-B), then correct in Column E
form1099B_basisReconciliation.irsCP2000Risk: Failing to adjust basis is the most common cause of IRS CP2000 notices for equity compensation

multiStateAllocation.note: Employees working in multiple states during vesting period must allocate equity income by state
multiStateAllocation.rsuMethod: Days worked in each state / total days in vesting period x RSU income = each state's allocation
multiStateAllocation.isoNsoMethod: Same proration applied to ordinary income at exercise
multiStateAllocation.remoteWorkTrap: California employee who moved to Texas before vesting may still owe CA tax on CA-period portion

combinedSalaryCalculator._purpose: Single calculator that models total household tax burden across all income types simultaneously. Produces one unified tax bill, quarterly payment schedule, and withholding optimization recommendation.

w2_salary.taxTreatment: Ordinary income. FICA withheld by employer.

rsu_vesting.taxTreatment: W-2 ordinary income at FMV on vest date. Supplemental withholding 22% (under $1M)

iso_exercise.taxTreatment: No regular income tax. AMT preference = bargain element.

nso_exercise.taxTreatment: Spread = ordinary income. W-2 if employee, SE tax if contractor.

espp_sale.qualifyingOrdinaryIncome: min(0.15 x fmv_at_offering, sale_price - purchase_price) x shares

qsbs_sale.exclusionCap: max(15000000 if post-July-2025 else 10000000, 10 x adjusted_basis)

quarterlyEstimatedEngine._purpose: Compute quarterly payment amounts for combined household income — all sources aggregated

annualizedIncomeMethod.description: For lumpy income (large RSU vest in Q3, QSBS sale in Q4) use Form 2210 Schedule AI — base each quarter's payment on actual income earned that quarter
annualizedIncomeMethod.useWhen: Income is not evenly distributed across quarters
annualizedIncomeMethod.benefit: Avoids overpaying early quarters when income materializes late in year

underpaymentPenalty.avoidedBy: Meet either safe harbor rule OR pay 90% of actual current-year tax

tech_couple_MFJ.label: Tech couple: dual W-2 + RSU + ISO exercise + ESPP
keyRisks: SS wage base tracked per W-2 but Additional Medicare applies on combined MFJ income over $250K
ISO AMT modeled on combined MFJ return — not individual spouse income
ESPP qualifying disposition ordinary income not withheld — add to quarterly payment
RSU withholding at 22% under-covers 32%+ bracket — calculate gap per paycheck
tech_couple_MFJ.quarterlyStrategy: Increase RSU spouse W-4 additional withholding by gap divided by remaining pay periods. ESPP gap paid as one-time Q3 estimated payment.

founder_MFJ_QSBS.label: Founder: moderate salary + QSBS sale event
keyRisks: Federal: QSBS gain 100% excluded — effective federal rate near 0% on gain
California: 13.3% on full gain amount — no CA conformity. $15M gain = $1,995,000 CA tax.
CA quarterly payments required — cannot defer to April
QSBS stacking: gifting shares to spouse and children before sale multiplies exclusion caps
founder_MFJ_QSBS.quarterlyStrategy: Pay California estimated tax on gain in quarter of sale. Federal: no quarterly on excluded amount.

w2_plus_gig_family.label: W-2 spouse + 1099 gig spouse + children employed in business
keyRisks: SE spouse owes SE tax on net gig income — not covered by spouse1 W-2 withholding
Children wages FICA-exempt if sole prop or SMLLC under age 18
Child wages up to $14,600 sheltered by standard deduction — near-zero tax on child income
QBI deduction applies to SE spouse net income if under threshold
w2_plus_gig_family.quarterlyStrategy: SE spouse pays quarterly estimated taxes independently. Model W-2 spouse increasing W-4 withholding to cover SE spouse gap if easier administratively.

w2_rsu_rental_ssdi_family.label: W-2 + RSU + rental + SSDI family member
keyRisks: SSDI: half of SSDI + other income = combined income. If above $44K MFJ → up to 85% of SSDI taxable
RSU: 22% withheld vs 32% actual bracket → withholding gap per vest
Rental: passive income on Schedule E, no SE tax, passive loss limits apply
Additional Medicare: total W-2 + RSU + SSDI taxable portion may exceed $250K MFJ threshold
w2_rsu_rental_ssdi_family.quarterlyStrategy: Increase W-4 withholding to cover RSU gap. Verify rental and SSDI income not creating underpayment.

allIncomeTypes_complex.label: Complex family: W-2 + RSU + 1099 consulting + ESPP + rental + crypto
allIncomeTypes_complex.note: Most complex scenario — calculator provides estimate. Recommend CPA for actual filing due to interaction of multiple income types on NIIT threshold, AMT, passive loss rules, and SE tax.
allIncomeTypes_complex.quarterlyStrategy: Annualized income method (Form 2210 AI) recommended. Model each quarter separately as equity and consulting income materializes.

withholdingOptimizationTool._purpose: Help households eliminate both underpayment penalty risk and large April surprise by calibrating W-4 and quarterly payments to expected total tax
seoKeywords: how much to withhold W-4 RSU
quarterly estimated tax calculator combined income
combined income tax calculator W-2 1099 RSU 2026
tech employee total compensation tax calculator
stock options W-2 combined tax calculator
quarterly estimated tax calculator all income types
startup equity and salary tax calculator
how much tax on RSU plus salary
